headings: h2: WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON
text_excerpt: About – JEFF SMITH MP JEFF SMITH MP WORKING HARD FOR MANCHESTER WITHINGTON Menu Skip to content Home About Parliament PMQs & Parliamentary Tours Drug Law Reform Mental Health Environment Policies Assisted Dying Bill Israel, Palestine & Lebanon Help & Advice Cost of Living Crisis Cost of Living Crisis Energy Support Debt Advice Mortgage repayments Emergency Support Finances, Social Security & Employment Housing Immigration & Asylum Local Councillors Contact Privacy Policy Your Local Election Candidates for May 2026 Cllr Suzannah Reeves – Old Moat Ward Ben Williams – Didsbury West Ward Cllr Joanna Midgley – Chorlton Park Ward Cllr John Hacking – Chorlton Ward Cllr Andrew Simcock – Didsbury East Ward Cllr Angela Gartside – Withington Ward About I’m proud to be the Labour MP for Manchester Withington. I was born and raised in Withington, and it’s a privilege to represent the people of my local area in Parliament. I was elected to Parliament in 2015, and prior to that I served as a local Councillor in my home ward of Old Moat, becoming the Council’s Executive Member for Education, for Finance and for Housing & Regeneration. I saw how Tory budget cuts damaged local services but also how local government could provide innovative solutions when the government in Westminster cut back investment in our communities. Before becoming an MP, I had a career in event management and as a DJ in clubs and colleges across the north of England, seeing first-hand the important role music has played in Manchester’s history and culture. In Parliament, I served in the opposition whips office, as well as in Shadow Ministerial posts in the Local Government, Culture Media & Sport, and Energy & Net Zero teams. From July 2024 to September 2025, I served as a Senior Government Whip. As your MP, I have an office in the heart of the constituency, at Withington Fire Station, as well as one in Parliament. My team are always available to support residents with problems. I run advice surgeries by appointment, and I am always keen to visit local community groups, schools and social activities. If you’d like me to visit a local school or community organisation, please get in touch with my office. Away from politics, I’m a lifelong Manchester City season ticket holder and take every opportunity I can to see Bruce Springsteen in concert. Please don’t hesitate to get in touch with me with any questions, or for advice and support. You can email me at jeff.smith.mp@parliament.uk or phone our constituency office on 0161 445 0678.
